summary: Alphabet is the strongest AI infrastructure play among hyperscalers, with Google Cloud accelerating to 63% growth and a $460B backlog justifying its $180-190B capex commitment; the market has validated this with a 10% surge post-earnings.

Meta is the most vulnerable of the four hyperscalers to continued market skepticism because it lacks a third-party cloud business to validate its massive capex commitment, making its AI spending thesis the weakest among peers.

The oil futures curve is pricing a sharp normalization from current ~$110 Brent to roughly $80 by December 2026 and ~$70 by late 2027, representing either a generational hedging opportunity for energy buyers or a violent correction risk if the backwardation proves wrong.

The S&P 500 is in a constructive trend with the path of least resistance higher into quarter-end, supported by record retail buying flows and favorable July seasonal tailwinds, with 7,610 as the key level to clear.
Front-end Treasury yields face upward pressure with the risk of accelerating higher if the May PCE print surprises to the upside, as the market would move to fully price the 2026 rate hike.
